title: Sign approved for immediate installation
text: The chamber of commerce announced that the electric sign, which had been under controversy for the past year, was to be erected immediately.
open_questions: Was the sign actually installed following this announcement?

title: Proposed location: corner of San Dimas Avenue and Bonita Avenue
text: The proposed location for the sign was the corner of San Dimas Avenue and Bonita Avenue in San Dimas.
open_questions: Was the sign installed at this exact corner, and was final placement ever confirmed?

title: Sign donated by William McNeil of Charter Oak
text: The sign was donated to the community by William McNeil of Charter Oak.
open_questions: What else is documented about William McNeil beyond this donation?

title: Sign described as a large illuminated rectangle
text: The sign was described as a large illuminated rectangle bearing the name of the city in large letters.
open_questions: Do any photographs, drawings, or advertisements confirm this description?

title: Campbell and Belknap appointed to installation committee
text: John Campbell and L. R. Belknap were appointed to a committee to attend to the installation of the sign.
open_questions: Did Campbell and Belknap complete the installation, and do any records document their work?

title: Estimated electricity cost of approximately $10 per month
text: The installation committee reported that electricity for the sign would cost the chamber of commerce approximately $10 each month.
open_questions: Do chamber of commerce financial records confirm ongoing electricity payments at this or any rate?

metadata.note: Reported as a cost estimate at the time, not a confirmed ongoing expense.

title: Diagonal placement discussed as a possibility
text: The article reports that the sign's position was discussed, with a possibility of placing it diagonally at the corner so that it might be seen from both streets.
open_questions: Was the sign ultimately placed diagonally, or in another orientation?

metadata.note: Reported as a discussed possibility, not a final decision.
